pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly writes these targets to the observing plan text file, and verifier.py checks for their presence.
setup_task.sh specifies Ha and OIII filters in the observing protocol, and verifier.py explicitly counts FITS files with these filters.
setup_task.sh requires 60-second exposures, and verifier.py enforces a minimum exposure time of 59.0 seconds.
setup_task.sh creates this directory and export_result.sh uses it as BASE_DIR.
setup_task.sh instructs the agent to write the report here, and export_result.sh reads from this path.
setup_task.sh dynamically creates the observing plan at this exact path.
Subjective descriptive label without a verifiable source in the environment.
Subjective descriptive label without a verifiable source in the environment.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates in a simulated environment using synthetic FITS imagery from the KStars/INDI CCD simulator. The observing plan is synthetically generated by setup_task.sh and specifies three targets (WR 136, WR 7, WR 134), two filters (Ha, OIII), and an exposure time of 60 seconds. The expected output directories (/home/ga/Images/WR_survey) and file paths for the plan and report exactly match the task description and verifier logic.
